data.title: McDonald's looks to ride the Grimace Effect if the New York Mets keep winning
data.description:
McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) is looking to make the most out of the post-season run of the New York Mets, with its Grimace character having a pop culture moment.

Grimace has been considered a good luck charm for the baseball club since the McDonald's (MCD) character threw out the first pitch before a game in June when the team was struggling.

The restaurant operator has pushed the Grimace-Mets connection out on its social media accounts, NYC subway cars, and billboards. The New York Mets have also been using Grimace in the team's Instagram posts, and even added a special purple seat at Citi Field to honor his special connection to Mets fans. Grimace has also been featured prominently in Times Square and rode the subway to watch Game 3 of the National League Division Series against the Phillies.

The "Grimace Effect" has moved the meter several times in the past with various McDonald's (MCD) promotions and inspired the restaurant giant to develop a new small store format called CosMc's, which is a reference to a McDonaldland mascot who appeared in advertisements in the late 1980s and early 1990s. The return of the CosMc character in 2023 followed the social media viral success with the Grimace Birthday Meal that led to widespread exposure for the brand in general. Analysts have noted several other Grimace promotions that have helped drive restaurant traffic in spurts.

data.title: Wave stock soars 73% on positive data for RNA editing treatment (update)
data.description:
Wave Life Sciences (NASDAQ:WVE) shares soared 73% Wednesday afternoon after the company announced positive proof-of-mechanism data from its Phase 1b/2a RestoraAATion-2 study for its RNA editing treatment candidate WVE-006 for alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, or AATD.

WVE-006 was developed using Wave’s oligonucleotide chemistry platform and is designed to address AATD-related lung disease and/or liver disease. Wave said the proof-of-mechanism data are “the first-ever clinical demonstration of RNA editing in humans,” according to a statement.

The company expects to release multi-dose data from the study next year.

U.K. drugmaker GSK (NYSE:GSK) holds the exclusive global license for WVE-006 and will assume development and commercialization responsibilities for the product after Wave wraps up the RestorAATion-2 study. Wave said it is eligible to receive up to $525M in milestone payments plus tiered royalties on net sales for WVE-006 under the GSK agreement.

Wave added that it plans to highlight the WVE-006 data along with data from its GalNAc-RNA editing programs and preclinical data for its obesity drug candidate WVE-007 during its Annual Research Day on Oct. 30.

Late Tuesday, Wave said in an SEC filing that Takeda (TAK) has decided not to exercise its option on a clinical candidate for Huntington's disease, WVE-003. Wave plans to continue development on the product.

Wave's WVE-006 news also stoked shares of two other RNA editing companies, Korro Bio (KRRO) and ProQR Therapeutics (PRQR). Korro (KRRO) shares soared 114% in afternoon trading while ProQR (PRQR) shares shot up 98%.

(Updates with recent stock movement, adds Korro Bio and ProQR.)

data.title: Druckenmiller: A Trump win would give equities a 3- to 6-month rally before a snuff out
data.description:
Billionaire investor Stanley Druckenmiller said that the stock market is convinced that Donald Trump is going to win the presidency this November.

The chairman and CEO of the Duquesne Family office said in a Bloomberg interview that when looking at market indicators for the economy, it seems like it is a win for Trump.

“You can see it in the bank stocks (KBE), (KBWB), (KRE), you can see it in crypto (BITQ), (FDIG),” he said. “You can see it in his Trump Media & Technology (DJT) social media company.”

Druckenmiller said that throughout the markets, industries that are expecting deregulation would benefit from a Trump win or would outperform others.

He said that a “blue sweep” is “extremely unlikely,” and that a “red sweep” seems more plausible.

“In a ‘red sweep’ you get animal spirits in the business community; you get deregulation and there might be some sort of uplift [in the business community] relative to where they were,” he said.

He added that the economy could be potentially stronger during the first three to six months, but that “my fear would be – because of those reasons, and because I think bond yields (SPLB), (GBF), (SPIB), (BSV) already don’t reflect the proper economic outlook – you’ll probably get a bad response from the fixed income markets (DFCF), (FIXD), which could then snuff out the equity rally.”

Also, under a red sweep, the Fed would be “much more hawkish than they would be under a Harris administration,” he said.

However, he reiterated that he doesn’t support either one of them. He said he finds Kamala Harris’s policies “anti-business,” but he said he doesn’t see Trump any better.

“Both of them think, apparently, that the government should have a major role in allocating capital, which I find frankly bizarre.”

data.title: Wolfspeed nominates two directors amid activist Jana push
data.description:
Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF) said it nominated two directors to its board for election at the annual meeting in December.

The silicon carbide maker nominated Thomas Seifert and Woody Young for board election at the company's annual meeting on Dec. 5, according to a statement earlier Wednesday.

The board nominations come as activist Jana Partners has a sizeable stake in Wolfspeed (WOLF) and has reportedly pushed for the company to explore it to explore its options, including a potential sale. The activist push comes after Jana first disclosed a stake in Wolfspeed (WOLF) in November of last year.

“We applaud the additions of Woody and Tom to the board and believe they will enhance the board’s efforts to improve operational execution and unlock Wolfspeed’s strategic value,” Scott Ostfeld, JANA’s Managing Partner, said in a statement emailed to Seeking Alpha.

Shares of Wolfspeed (WOLF) surged 21% on Tuesday and climbed another 16% on Wednesday after the Biden-Harris administration announced plans to provide up to $750M in direct funding to the EV chipmaker to support the expansion of silicon carbide manufacturing in the U.S.

data.title: Bankrupt Gritstone bio in funding talks
data.description:
• Gritstone bio (NASDAQ:GRTS), which filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection on Oct. 10, is having talks with parties that could provide funding.
  • "We have had some conversations with several parties that might be interested in being a plan sponsor,” Debra Grassgreen, a lawyer representing the company, said at a Wednesday bankruptcy court hearing, Bloomberg reported. She added such a financial lifeline could "keep the company alive."
  • A judge has also approved a Gritstone request to use cash collateral to pay wages and vendors.

data.title: SpaceX sues California regulator, claiming political bias against launches
data.description:
SpaceX (SPACE) on Tuesday sued regulators in California after they rejected the space exploration company’s request to permit more rocket launches, claiming their decision was motivated by politics.

Last week, the California Coastal Commission denied a U.S. Space Force request to increase the number of launches of SpaceX’s (SPACE) Falcon 9 rocket from 36 to 50 a year from Vandenberg Space Force Base near Santa Barbara, Calif. In a 6-4 vote, the commission cited their previous requests that the military and SpaceX (SPACE) lessen the sonic booms caused by the rockets and to monitor the effects on wildlife.

SpaceX’s (SPACE) 284-page lawsuit accuses regulators of overreaching their authority and violating the right to free speech of SpaceX (SPACE) founder Elon Musk.

The lawsuit is the latest run-in between Musk and California after years of bickering over government policy, tax breaks and labor laws.

Before the commission voted at their Thursday meeting, Commissioner Gretchen Newsom cited Musk’s statements on social media about this year’s presidential election and alleged that he spread misleading statements about Hurricane Helene.

“Right now Elon Musk is hopping about the country, spewing and tweeting political falsehoods and attacking FEMA while claiming his desire to help the hurricane victims with free Starlink access to the internet,” Newsom said.

She isn’t related to California Gov. Gavin Newsom.

“Rarely has a government agency made so clear that it was exceeding its authorized mandate to punish a company for the political views and statements of its largest shareholder and CEO,” SpaceX (SPACE) claimed in its suit.

The commission’s vote against the launches may be meaningless because SpaceX (SPACE) contracts services to the federal government, including the military. Pentagon officials said SpaceX’s (SPACE) rocket launches should be considered a federal activity, The Wall Street Journal reported on Wednesday.

data.title: Sherwin Williams declares $0.715 dividend
data.description:
• Sherwin Williams (NYSE:SHW) declares $0.715/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.74%
  • Payable Dec. 6; for shareholders of record Nov. 15; ex-div Nov. 15.

data.title: No more shrinkflation? PepsiCo says it will add more potato chips to some bags
data.description:
PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) plans to put more Lay’s, Doritos, Tostitos and Ruffles potato chips in some bags after hearing criticism about the reduced size by weight of individual bags over the last two years. The so-called "bonus" bags will contain 20% more chips for the same price as standard bags in select locations. PepsiCo (PEP) is also adding two additional small chip bags to its variety-pack option with 18 bags.

During PepsiCo's (PEP) Q3 earnings conference call (transcript), CEO Ramon LAguarta said the food and beverage giant wants to use the fall and winter to put more investments behind the Doritos and Tostitos brands in general.

"It’s the football season, there are a lot of gatherings, and those brands belong very well in those gatherings. And it will be a combination of value in the form of bonus packs and more product too. Obviously, these are large -- normally large group gatherings, so bonus packs make a lot of sense. We're giving 20% more product in Tostitos and Ruffles, some of the brands that belong in those locations."

It is unclear if the company will keep the promotion going well into 2025.

data.title: Klaviyo initiated at Equal Weight after stock hits record high: Wells Fargo
data.description:
Klaviyo (NYSE:KVYO) was initiated with an Equal Weight rating by Wells Fargo as it is considered a leader in the small to medium business automated marketing sector.

Wells Fargo also set a price target of $37 on the stock. Klaviyo has surged 53% over the past six months, closing at an all-time high of $37.42 on Tuesday. It was down 2% during early afternoon trading on Wednesday.

Klaviyo offers a marketing automation platform that utilizes eCommerce SMS and email marketing to help businesses acquire, retain and grow their customer base.

"KVYO's data layer origins provides a foundation to deliver real time omni-channel marketing capabilities, while simultaneously obfuscating data complexities for SMBs and mid-market companies, a highly unique combo," said Wells Fargo analysts, led by Michael Berg, in a Wednesday investor note.

It also operates in a sector with a total addressable market of about $50B. However, it is also in a highly competitive market facing near-term macroeconomic headwinds, Berg cautioned.

Klaviyo is slated to release its third quarter financial results on November 7 after markets close. A consensus of analysts expects the company to report adjusted earnings per share of $0.11 on revenue of $226.24M.

data.title: Universal Health Services upgraded at TD Cowen on state direct payment expansion
data.description:
TD Cowen has upgraded Universal Health Services (NYSE:UHS) to buy from hold citing the expansion of hospital state directed payments (SDPs) in several states.

The firm upped its price target to $283 from $220 (~26% upside based on Oct. 15 close).

Analyst Ryan Langston noted the company's consensus EBITDA could increase by 15%-20% over the next three to 12 months thanks to the increase in SDPs.

"We believe future SDP EBITDA upside is underappreciated by investors, both in terms of durability and magnitude," he wrote. "The impact from SDP to the hospital narrative is likely to outweigh potential near/intermediate term return to a normalized utilization and cost trend environment in our view."

Langston added that CMS is likely to finalize SDP enhancements in Tennessee and the District of Columbia by the end of the year. The same is expected to happen in Texas through state rulemaking. CMS enhancements in Florida and California could happen by the end of 2025, he noted.
